creator: fabian@wenks.ch
text:
(In reply to Miroslav Lachman from comment #9)

My /usr/local/etc/mysql/my.cnf contains the proper socket path. As I never have touched it. It was installed with the upgrade to MariaDB 10.6, as the file date is current.

And also 'mariadb --print-defaults' shows '--socket=/var/run/mysql/mysql.sock'.

I did only adjust /usr/local/etc/mysql/conf.d/server.cnf, as the my.cnf in the datadir (default in /var/db/mysql, in my case /export/data1/mysql as I have set that in rc.conf since ever) seems to be ignored now.

Also the ~/.my.cnf from root only contains [client] with user and password (I know, would not be needed any more with 10.6).
